# OpenMDAO easy: file utility basics

Wrap concrete `openmdao.utils.file_utils` helpers and inspect one checked-in Python module like a real utility workflow.

## Why This Case Exists

The easiest OpenMDAO challenge should start from stable utility functions and still do recognizable file-oriented work instead of stopping at import smoke.

## Case Shape

- ID: `openmdao_easy`
- Family: `openmdao`
- Difficulty: `easy`
- Tier: `gha_subset`
- Style: `package`
- Workflow Slice: `systems`
- Target Kind: `package`
- Upstream Target: `openmdao.utils.file_utils`
- Catalog Source: `challenges/cases/openmdao_easy/challenge.toml`

## Ingestion Breadth

- Minimum generated tools: `2`
- Required generated tools: `fname2mod_name`, `is_python_file`

## Why This Stays A Challenge

This case keeps its ingestion recipe and workflow assertions in
`challenge.toml` instead of checking in companion `ingest.py` and
`use.py` scripts. That boundary is intentional: the challenge lane
should stay a compact problem statement rather than turning into a
worked answer key like the real-world example lane.

## Run This Case

Use the convenience target:

```bash
make challenge CASE=openmdao_easy
```

Or call the runner directly:

```bash
PYTHONPATH=src .venv/bin/python scripts/run_challenges.py --catalog-dir challenges/cases --tier all --only openmdao_easy
```

## Prepared Inputs

This case does not need rendered setup inputs.

## Fixtures

- `challenges/cases/openmdao_easy/fixtures/demo_pkg/__init__.py`
- `challenges/cases/openmdao_easy/fixtures/demo_pkg/core.py`

## Workflow

### 1. Confirm the checked-in demo module is a Python file

Tool: `is_python_file`

Arguments:

```json
{
  "file_path": "{challenge_fixture_dir}/demo_pkg/core.py"
}
```

Expectations: text contains ['true'].

### 2. Derive the module basename from the checked-in demo module

Tool: `fname2mod_name`

Arguments:

```json
{
  "fname": "{challenge_fixture_dir}/demo_pkg/core.py"
}
```

Expectations: text contains ['core'].

## What This Case Proves

- Installed OpenMDAO utility helpers can be scaffolded deterministically in one shot.
- The generated facade can inspect a real checked-in Python module without handwritten glue.

## Challenge Definition

```toml
# Self-contained case: the shallowest useful OpenMDAO utility workflow.
# This easy rung stays on concrete file utilities and checked-in Python files.
id = "openmdao_easy"
title = "OpenMDAO easy: file utility basics"
tier = "gha_subset"
style = "package"
slice = "systems"
family = "openmdao"
difficulty = "easy"

[example]
summary = "Wrap concrete `openmdao.utils.file_utils` helpers and inspect one checked-in Python module like a real utility workflow."
motivation = "The easiest OpenMDAO challenge should start from stable utility functions and still do recognizable file-oriented work instead of stopping at import smoke."
proves = [
  "Installed OpenMDAO utility helpers can be scaffolded deterministically in one shot.",
  "The generated facade can inspect a real checked-in Python module without handwritten glue.",
]

[target]
kind = "package"
value = "openmdao.utils.file_utils"

[probe]
imports = ["openmdao.utils.file_utils"]

[scaffold]
kind = "package"
symbol_include_patterns = ["^fname2mod_name$", "^is_python_file$"]

[ingestion]
min_generated_tools = 2
required_tools = ["fname2mod_name", "is_python_file"]

[workflow]
[[workflow.steps]]
label = "Confirm the checked-in demo module is a Python file"
tool = "is_python_file"
arguments = { file_path = "{challenge_fixture_dir}/demo_pkg/core.py" }

[workflow.steps.expect]
text_contains = ["true"]

[[workflow.steps]]
label = "Derive the module basename from the checked-in demo module"
tool = "fname2mod_name"
arguments = { fname = "{challenge_fixture_dir}/demo_pkg/core.py" }

[workflow.steps.expect]
text_contains = ["core"]
```
